title: Investor og Novo-forfatter: Opsplit Novo - det vil løse krisen
summary_md:
# Investor og Novo-forfatter: Opsplit Novo - det vil løse krisen

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-04-20  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A Danish investor podcast focused on the healthcare sector and a deep post-mortem of Novo Nordisk's fall from grace, anchored by a new book about the company. The hosts argue healthcare is historically cheap (P/E ~17 vs S&P 500 ~20) but lacks momentum, while Novo faces a 'winner-takes-all' GLP-1 market eroding its moat as Eli Lilly and a wave of competitors arrive. The fund manager has exited Novo and Lilly, preferring value-unlock plays like GN Store Nord and aesthetics-focused AbbVie.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟡 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Fund manager exited Novo; analyst consensus heavily 'hold' (10 buy / 24 hold / 3 sell). Moat eroded, price cuts and copyists, flat scripts despite cheap P/E ~11.

#### Reasoning

Klaus sold out and will only re-enter on proof US prescriptions turn and grow again. The GLP-1 market is becoming winner-takes-all yet floods with competitors (Lilly, Pfizer, Amgen, AstraZeneca), forcing 80-90% price drops in the volume game. Earnings guided to decline in 2026; if no growth returns the stock could re-rate down to P/E 7-8 like flat-earnings pharma. Brand and positioning for the volume game remain genuine strengths.

#### Sell Triggers

- if US Wegovy/Ozempic prescriptions keep declining or stay flat
- if 2026 revenue falls below ~300bn DKK
- if GLP-1 prices fall faster than volume rises
- if competitor phase 3 readouts (Amgen, Pfizer, AstraZeneca) outperform

> *"Resultater... jag ska simpelvis se att vi går vid recepten begynner att vända i USA och växa igen det ska jag se"*

---

### 🟢 GN — GN Store Nord | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Klaus sees significant value: GN Hearing sale priced above GN's entire market cap, off-balance-sheet US stakes worth billions, plus Amplifon equity stake; awaiting capital allocation.

#### Reasoning

The pending sale of GN Hearing to Amplifon is worth more than all of GN today, with additional hidden value in footnoted US ownership stakes. New management can unlock value if it proves shareholder-friendly capital discipline, ending the historic pattern of expensive acquisitions. Klaus expects GN to be broken down and look very different in five years; market distrust of the cash pile is the key overhang to resolve.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the GN Hearing/Amplifon deal fails to close
- if management redeploys cash into expensive acquisitions
- if hidden US stakes are not realized/synliggjort

> *"nu ser jag extremt mycket värde i GN-aktien"*

---

### 🟢 ABBV — AbbVie | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Held in the fund; praised for purely aesthetics-focused obesity approach and Gubra partnership; cited as a company that genuinely does different things well.

#### Reasoning

Klaus holds AbbVie and was impressed in management meetings by its sharp focus on aesthetics rather than chasing cardiovascular benefit, alongside its Gubra collaboration. It exemplifies the diversified strategic options in the obesity space beyond the medical-weight-loss angle.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the Gubra collaboration underperforms
- if aesthetics positioning loses to medical-benefit competitors

> *"jag kikar mycket mot Abwee. Vi har Abwee på det före och de har lagt samarbete med Gubra"*

---

### 🟠 GUBRA — Gubra | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Danish biotech with an AbbVie collaboration on obesity/aesthetics, mentioned favourably as part of the AbbVie thesis.

#### Reasoning

Gubra is positively framed via its AbbVie partnership in the aesthetics-focused obesity segment, but no direct conviction or position was stated, making it a watch rather than an actionable buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the AbbVie collaboration is terminated
- if pipeline milestones slip

> *"de har lagt samarbete med Gubra"*

---

### 🟠 LLY — Eli Lilly | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Framed as the winner of the GLP-1 race via fast-follower advantage and harder-to-copy dual molecule; products growing in US. Fund does not hold it.

#### Reasoning

Lilly extracted huge value being the number-two fast follower, gaining US market share with a dual-molecule that copyists find harder to replicate; Bonjaro/Z-Bound are growing. However the fund holds neither Novo nor Lilly, and the whole class faces price pressure as more entrants arrive, so the view is constructive but observational.

#### Sell Triggers

- if obesity price war compresses margins faster than volume growth
- if next-generation competitor molecules leapfrog Lilly

> *"där må vi säga Elalisa fick riktigt mycket ut av att vara nummer två"*

---

### 🟠 GREENM — Green Mobility | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Year's best Danish-market investment; shares rose sharply after a turnaround, refocusing car-sharing on Copenhagen and winding down loss-making foreign operations.

#### Reasoning

Green Mobility staged a strong rebound after years of cash-burning growth (450m DKK raised), pivoting from aggressive international roll-out to a concentrated, more disciplined Copenhagen operation. Cited retrospectively as a successful pitch case rather than with forward conviction, so a watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Copenhagen-focused operations fail to reach profitability
- if it resumes cash-burning expansion

> *"Green Mobility som ju var årets investering på det danska aktiemarkedet sista året"*

---
